control_title: Verify that all user stories and features contain functional security constraints, such as "As a user, I should be able to view and edit my profile. I should not be able to view or edit anyone else's profile"
hub_name: Constrain functional features based on user stories
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Constrain functional features based on user stories

control_title: Verify documentation and justification of all the application's trust boundaries, components, and significant data flows.
hub_name: Document all trust boundaries and significant data flows
hub_path: Development processes for security > Technical system documentation > Document all trust boundaries and significant data flows

control_title: Verify definition and security analysis of the application's high-level architecture and all connected remote services.
hub_name: Describe high-level system architecture and perform threat modeling on it every critical change and regularly
hub_path: Development processes for security > Architecture/design processes > Describe high-level system architecture and perform threat modeling on it every critical change and regularly

control_title: Verify implementation of centralized, simple (economy of design), vetted, secure, and reusable security controls to avoid duplicate, missing, ineffective, or insecure controls.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure implemented architecture > Use centralized reusable security controls

control_title: Verify availability of a secure coding checklist, security requirements, guideline, or policy to all developers and testers.
hub_name: Make (centrally) available secure coding resources for programmers
hub_path: Development processes for security > Technical instructions > Make (centrally) available secure coding resources for programmers

control_title: Verify that a source code control system is in use, with procedures to ensure that check-ins are accompanied by issues or change tickets. The source code control system should have access control and identifiable users to allow traceability of any changes.
hub_name: Use source code control system with change traceability and access control
hub_path: Development processes for security > Configuration Management > Developer Configuration Management > Use source code control system with change traceability and access control

control_title: Verify the definition and documentation of all application components in terms of the business or security functions they provide.
hub_name: Documentation of all components' business or security function
hub_path: Development processes for security > Technical system documentation > Documentation of all components' business or security function

control_title: Verify that all high-value business logic flows, including authentication, session management and access control, do not share unsynchronized state.
hub_name: Do not share unsynchronized state on high-value logic flows
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Robust business logic > Parallel execution robustness > Do not share unsynchronized state on high-value logic flows

control_title: Verify that all high-value business logic flows, including authentication, session management and access control are thread safe and resistant to time-of-check and time-of-use race conditions.
hub_name: Ensure business flows' thread safety/resistance to race conditions
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Robust business logic > Parallel execution robustness > Ensure business flows' thread safety/resistance to race conditions

control_title: Verify that user-uploaded files - if required to be displayed or downloaded from the application - are served by either octet stream downloads, or from an unrelated domain, such as a cloud file storage bucket. Implement a suitable Content Security Policy (CSP) to reduce the risk from XSS vectors or other attacks from the uploaded file.
hub_name: Store and serve user-uploaded files such that they cannot execute/damage server or client
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File upload > Store and serve user-uploaded files such that they cannot execute/damage server or client

control_title: Verify the segregation of components of differing trust levels through well-defined security controls, firewall rules, API gateways, reverse proxies, cloud-based security groups, or similar mechanisms.
hub_name: Segregate components of differing trust levels
hub_path: Operating processes for security > Facilities management > Network security > Segregate components of differing trust levels

control_title: Verify that binary signatures, trusted connections, and verified endpoints are used to deploy binaries to remote devices.
hub_path: Development processes for security > Deploy/build > Check binary integrity before deployment

control_title: Verify that the build pipeline warns of out-of-date or insecure components and takes appropriate actions.
hub_name: Force build pipeline to check outdated/insecure components
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency management > Force build pipeline to check outdated/insecure components

control_title: Verify that the build pipeline contains a build step to automatically build and verify the secure deployment of the application, particularly if the application infrastructure is software defined, such as cloud environment build scripts.
hub_name: Automate secure build and deployment, especially with SDI
hub_path: Development processes for security > Deploy/build > Automate secure build and deployment, especially with SDI

control_title: Verify that application deployments adequately sandbox, containerize and/or isolate at the network level to delay and deter attackers from attacking other applications, especially when they are performing sensitive or dangerous actions such as deserialization.
hub_name: Sandbox, containerize and/or isolate applications at the network level
hub_path: Operating processes for security > Facilities management > Network security > Sandbox, containerize and/or isolate applications at the network level

control_title: Verify the application does not use unsupported, insecure, or deprecated client-side technologies such as NSAPI plugins, Flash, Shockwave, ActiveX, Silverlight, NACL, or client-side Java applets.
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Disallow unsupported/deprecated client-side technologies

control_title: Verify the use of unique or special low-privilege operating system accounts for all application components, services, and servers.
hub_name: Use least privilege OS accounts for system (components)
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Minimize permissions > Use least privilege OS accounts for system (components)

control_title: Verify that communications between application components, including APIs, middleware and data layers, are authenticated. Components should have the least necessary privileges needed.
hub_name: Mutually authenticate application components. Minimize privileges
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Authenticate consistently > Mutually authenticate application components. Minimize privileges

control_title: Verify that the application uses a single vetted authentication mechanism that is known to be secure, can be extended to include strong authentication, and has sufficient logging and monitoring to detect account abuse or breaches.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Use centralized authentication mechanism

control_title: Verify that all authentication pathways and identity management APIs implement consistent authentication security control strength, such that there are no weaker alternatives per the risk of the application.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Authenticate consistently > Consistently apply authentication strength

control_title: Verify that trusted enforcement points, such as access control gateways, servers, and serverless functions, enforce access controls. Never enforce access controls on the client.
hub_name: Enforce access control on trusted parts/serverside
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Enforce access control on trusted parts/serverside

control_title: Verify the application uses a single and well-vetted access control mechanism for accessing protected data and resources. All requests must pass through this single mechanism to avoid copy and paste or insecure alternative paths.
hub_name: Use a centralized access control mechanism
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Data access control > Use a centralized access control mechanism

control_title: Verify that attribute or feature-based access control is used whereby the code checks the user's authorization for a feature/data item rather than just their role. Permissions should still be allocated using roles.
hub_name: Use ABAC/FBAC on data/feature level, even when using RBAC for permissions
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Use ABAC/FBAC on data/feature level, even when using RBAC for permissions

control_title: Verify that input and output requirements clearly define how to handle and process data based on type, content, and applicable laws, regulations, and other policy compliance.
hub_path: Governance processes for security > Security Analysis and documentation > Security requirements > Clear policy compliant I/O requirements

control_title: Verify that serialization is not used when communicating with untrusted clients. If this is not possible, ensure that adequate integrity controls (and possibly encryption if sensitive data is sent) are enforced to prevent deserialization attacks including object injection.
hub_name: Block serialization of content from untrusted clients
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Deserialization Prevention > Block serialization of content from untrusted clients

control_title: Verify that input validation is enforced on a trusted service layer. ([C5](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))
hub_name: Enforce input validation on a trusted service layer
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Input validation > Enforce input validation on a trusted service layer

control_title: Verify that output encoding occurs close to or by the interpreter for which it is intended.
hub_name: Encode output near the consuming interpreter
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Output encoding and injection prevention > Encode output near the consuming interpreter

control_title: Verify that there is an explicit policy for management of cryptographic keys and that a cryptographic key lifecycle follows a key management standard such as NIST SP 800-57.
hub_path: Development processes for security > Technical system documentation > Document explicit key/secret management

control_title: Verify that consumers of cryptographic services protect key material and other secrets by using key vaults or API based alternatives.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Use key vaults

control_title: Verify that all keys and passwords are replaceable and are part of a well-defined process to re-encrypt sensitive data.
hub_name: Ensure keys and passwords are replaceable
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Ensure keys and passwords are replaceable

control_title: Verify that the architecture treats client-side secrets--such as symmetric keys, passwords, or API tokens--as insecure and never uses them to protect or access sensitive data.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Encrypt data at rest > Treat client-secrets as insecure

control_title: Verify that a common logging format and approach is used across the system.
hub_name: Log in consistent format across system
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Logging and error handling > Log integrity > Log in consistent format across system

control_title: Verify that logs are securely transmitted to a preferably remote system for analysis, detection, alerting, and escalation.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Logging and error handling > Log integrity > Securely transfer logs (remotely)

control_title: Verify that all sensitive data is identified and classified into protection levels.
hub_name: Classify sensitive data in protection levels
hub_path: Governance processes for security > Security Analysis and documentation > Asset management > Data classification and handling > Classify sensitive data in protection levels

control_title: Verify that all protection levels have an associated set of protection requirements, such as encryption requirements, integrity requirements, retention, privacy and other confidentiality requirements, and that these are applied in the architecture.
hub_path: Governance processes for security > Security Analysis and documentation > Asset management > Data classification and handling > Document requirements for (data) protection levels

control_title: Verify the application encrypts communications between components, particularly when these components are in different containers, systems, sites, or cloud providers.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure communication > Communication encryption > Protect communication between application components

control_title: Verify that application components verify the authenticity of each side in a communication link to prevent person-in-the-middle attacks. For example, application components should validate TLS certificates and chains.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure communication > Communication authentication > Mutually authenticate application components

control_title: Verify that a code analysis tool is in use that can detect potentially malicious code, such as time functions, unsafe file operations and network connections.
hub_name: Use static analysis tooling to detect potentially malicious actions
hub_path: Development processes for security > Verification > Automated static security analysis of code and configuration > Use static analysis tooling to detect potentially malicious actions

control_title: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain unauthorized phone home or data collection capabilities. Where such functionality exists, obtain the user's permission for it to operate before collecting any data.
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency integrity > Avoid unauthorized client data collection

control_title: Verify that the application does not ask for unnecessary or excessive permissions to privacy related features or sensors, such as contacts, cameras, microphones, or location.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Minimize permissions > Let application request minimal permissions

control_title: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain back doors, such as hard-coded or additional undocumented accounts or keys, code obfuscation, undocumented binary blobs, rootkits, or anti-debugging, insecure debugging features, or otherwise out of date, insecure, or hidden functionality that could be used maliciously if discovered.
hub_name: Check source code and third party libraries to not contain backdoors
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency integrity > Check source code and third party libraries to not contain backdoors

control_title: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain time bombs by searching for date and time related functions.
hub_name: Check source code and third party libraries to not contain timebombs
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency integrity > Check source code and third party libraries to not contain timebombs

control_title: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain malicious code, such as salami attacks, logic bypasses, or logic bombs.
hub_name: Check source code and third party libraries to not contain malicious code
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency integrity > Check source code and third party libraries to not contain malicious code

control_title: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain Easter eggs or any other potentially unwanted functionality.
hub_name: Harden application by excluding unwanted functionality
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Harden application by excluding unwanted functionality

control_title: Verify that if the application has a client or server auto-update feature, updates should be obtained over secure channels and digitally signed. The update code must validate the digital signature of the update before installing or executing the update.
hub_path: Development processes for security > Deploy/build > Secure auto-updates over full stack

control_title: Verify that the application employs integrity protections, such as code signing or subresource integrity. The application must not load or execute code from untrusted sources, such as loading includes, modules, plugins, code, or libraries from untrusted sources or the Internet.
hub_name: Allow only trusted sources both build time and runtime; therefore perform integrity checks on all resources and code
hub_path: Development processes for security > Deploy/build > Allow only trusted sources both build time and runtime; therefore perform integrity checks on all resources and code

control_title: Verify that the application has protection from subdomain takeovers if the application relies upon DNS entries or DNS subdomains, such as expired domain names, out of date DNS pointers or CNAMEs, expired projects at public source code repos, or transient cloud APIs, serverless functions, or storage buckets (*autogen-bucket-id*.cloud.example.com) or similar. Protections can include ensuring that DNS names used by applications are regularly checked for expiry or change.
hub_name: Ensure integrity of DNS entries and domains
hub_path: Operating processes for security > Facilities management > Network security > Ensure integrity of DNS entries and domains

control_title: Verify that the application will only process business logic flows for the same user in sequential step order and without skipping steps.
hub_name: Enforce natural sequence of business flows to avoid abuse
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Robust business logic > Detect and prevent unusual activity > Enforce natural sequence of business flows to avoid abuse

control_title: Verify that the application will only process business logic flows with all steps being processed in realistic human time, i.e. transactions are not submitted too quickly.
hub_name: Monitor for realistic "human time" business logic flows
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Robust business logic > Detect and prevent unusual activity > Monitor for realistic "human time" business logic flows

control_title: Verify the application has appropriate limits for specific business actions or transactions which are correctly enforced on a per user basis.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Limit/authorize user's access to functionality

control_title: Verify that the application has anti-automation controls to protect against excessive calls such as mass data exfiltration, business logic requests, file uploads or denial of service attacks.
hub_name: Detect and protect against automation abuse
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > Detect and protect against automation abuse

control_title: Verify the application has business logic limits or validation to protect against likely business risks or threats, identified using threat modeling or similar methodologies.
hub_name: Implement business logic limits against identified business risks
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Robust business logic > Detect and prevent unusual activity > Implement business logic limits against identified business risks

control_title: Verify that the application does not suffer from "Time Of Check to Time Of Use" (TOCTOU) issues or other race conditions for sensitive operations.
hub_name: Protect sensitive functionalities against race conditions
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Robust business logic > Parallel execution robustness > Protect sensitive functionalities against race conditions

control_title: Verify that the application monitors for unusual events or activity from a business logic perspective. For example, attempts to perform actions out of order or actions which a normal user would never attempt.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Robust business logic > Detect and prevent unusual activity > Monitor unusual activities on system

control_title: Verify that the application has configurable alerting when automated attacks or unusual activity is detected.
hub_name: Enable configurable alert against usage anomalies
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Robust business logic > Detect and prevent unusual activity > Enable configurable alert against usage anomalies

control_title: Verify that the application will not accept large files that could fill up storage or cause a denial of service.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File upload > Validate max input/file sizes

control_title: Verify that the application checks compressed files (e.g. zip, gz, docx, odt) against maximum allowed uncompressed size and against maximum number of files before uncompressing the file.
hub_name: Check uploaded archives for decompression attacks (eg zip bombs)
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File upload > Check uploaded archives for decompression attacks (eg zip bombs)

control_title: Verify that a file size quota and maximum number of files per user is enforced to ensure that a single user cannot fill up the storage with too many files, or excessively large files.
hub_name: Limit size and number of uploaded files
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File upload > Limit size and number of uploaded files

control_title: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are validated to be of expected type based on the file's content.
hub_name: Validate file type of data from untrusted sources
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > Validate file type of data from untrusted sources

control_title: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is not used directly by system or framework filesystems and that a URL API is used to protect against path traversal.
hub_name: Sanitize filename metadata from untrusted origin if processing is required
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File execution > Sanitize filename metadata from untrusted origin if processing is required

control_title: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is validated or ignored to prevent the disclosure, creation, updating or removal of local files (LFI).
hub_name: Ignore/at least validate  filename metadata from untrusted origin (local file context, eg LFI)
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File execution > Ignore/at least validate  filename metadata from untrusted origin (local file context, eg LFI)

control_title: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is validated or ignored to prevent the disclosure or execution of remote files via Remote File Inclusion (RFI) or Server-side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks.
hub_name: Ignore/at least validate  filename metadata from untrusted origin (remote file context, eg RFI)
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File execution > Ignore/at least validate  filename metadata from untrusted origin (remote file context, eg RFI)

control_title: Verify that the application protects against Reflective File Download (RFD) by validating or ignoring user-submitted filenames in a JSON, JSONP, or URL parameter, the response Content-Type header should be set to text/plain, and the Content-Disposition header should have a fixed filename.
hub_name: Ignore/at least validate filenames from untrusted origin (against RFD)
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File execution > Ignore/at least validate filenames from untrusted origin (against RFD)

control_title: Verify that untrusted file metadata is not used directly with system API or libraries, to protect against OS command injection.
hub_name: Block direct execution of file metadata from untrusted origin
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File execution > Block direct execution of file metadata from untrusted origin

control_title: Verify that the application does not include and execute functionality from untrusted sources, such as unverified content distribution networks, JavaScript libraries, node npm libraries, or server-side DLLs.
hub_name: Ignore/block execution logic from untrusted sources
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File execution > Ignore/block execution logic from untrusted sources

control_title: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are stored outside the web root, with limited permissions.
hub_name: Securely store files with untrusted origin
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File storage > Securely store files with untrusted origin

control_title: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are scanned by antivirus scanners to prevent upload and serving of known malicious content.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File upload > Scan untrusted files for malware

control_title: Verify that the web tier is configured to serve only files with specific file extensions to prevent unintentional information and source code leakage. For example, backup files (e.g. .bak), temporary working files (e.g. .swp), compressed files (.zip, .tar.gz, etc) and other extensions commonly used by editors should be blocked unless required.
hub_name: Whitelist file extensions served by web tier
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > File download > Whitelist file extensions served by web tier

control_title: Verify that direct requests to uploaded files will never be executed as HTML/JavaScript content.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > File handling > Block execution/output of uploaded files

control_title: Verify that the web or application server is configured with an allow list of resources or systems to which the server can send requests or load data/files from.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > Whitelist data sources and sinks

control_title: Verify that all application components use the same encodings and parsers to avoid parsing attacks that exploit different URI or file parsing behavior that could be used in SSRF and RFI attacks.
hub_name: Force uniform encoders and parsers throughout system
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > Force uniform encoders and parsers throughout system

control_title: Verify API URLs do not expose sensitive information, such as the API key, session tokens etc.
hub_name: Do not expose data through API URLs
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure communication > Minimize communication > Do not expose data through API URLs

control_title: Verify that authorization decisions are made at both the URI, enforced by programmatic or declarative security at the controller or router, and at the resource level, enforced by model-based permissions.
hub_name: Enforce model-based authorization both at URI and final resource
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Enforce model-based authorization both at URI and final resource

control_title: Verify that requests containing unexpected or missing content types are rejected with appropriate headers (HTTP response status 406 Unacceptable or 415 Unsupported Media Type).
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > Reject non-whitelisted content types

control_title: Verify that enabled RESTful HTTP methods are a valid choice for the user or action, such as preventing normal users using DELETE or PUT on protected API or resources.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > Limit REST HTTP methods

control_title: Verify that JSON schema validation is in place and verified before accepting input.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Input validation > Enforce JSON schema before processing

control_title: Verify that RESTful web services that utilize cookies are protected from Cross-Site Request Forgery via the use of at least one or more of the following: double submit cookie pattern, CSRF nonces, or Origin request header checks.
hub_name: Add CSRF protection for cookie based REST services
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > RESTful > Add CSRF protection for cookie based REST services

control_title: Verify that REST services explicitly check the incoming Content-Type to be the expected one, such as application/xml or application/json.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > RESTful > Verify content-type for REST services

control_title: Verify that the message headers and payload are trustworthy and not modified in transit. Requiring strong encryption for transport (TLS only) may be sufficient in many cases as it provides both confidentiality and integrity protection. Per-message digital signatures can provide additional assurance on top of the transport protections for high-security applications but bring with them additional complexity and risks to weigh against the benefits.
hub_name: Verify the authenticity of both headers and payload
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure communication > Communication encryption > Verify the authenticity of both headers and payload

control_title: Verify that XSD schema validation takes place to ensure a properly formed XML document, followed by validation of each input field before any processing of that data takes place.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > SOAP > Enforce schema on XML structure/field

control_title: Verify that the message payload is signed using WS-Security to ensure reliable transport between client and service.
hub_name: Add integrity check to SOAP payload
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > SOAP > Add integrity check to SOAP payload

control_title: Verify that a query allow list or a combination of depth limiting and amount limiting is used to prevent GraphQL or data layer expression Denial of Service (DoS) as a result of expensive, nested queries. For more advanced scenarios, query cost analysis should be used.
hub_name: Limit query impact GraphQL/data layer expression DoS
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Sanitization and sandboxing > Limit query impact GraphQL/data layer expression DoS

control_title: Verify that GraphQL or other data layer authorization logic should be implemented at the business logic layer instead of the GraphQL layer.
hub_name: Separate GraphQL (or similar) authorization logic from data layer
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > API/web services > Separate GraphQL (or similar) authorization logic from data layer

control_title: Verify that the application build and deployment processes are performed in a secure and repeatable way, such as CI / CD automation, automated configuration management, and automated deployment scripts.
hub_name: Securely automate build and deployment in pipeline
hub_path: Development processes for security > Deploy/build > Securely automate build and deployment in pipeline

control_title: Verify that compiler flags are configured to enable all available buffer overflow protections and warnings, including stack randomization, data execution prevention, and to break the build if an unsafe pointer, memory, format string, integer, or string operations are found.
hub_name: Use features in compile and build tools for executable security
hub_path: Development processes for security > Deploy/build > Use features in compile and build tools for executable security

control_title: Verify that server configuration is hardened as per the recommendations of the application server and frameworks in use.
hub_name: Proper Configuration for all applications and frameworks
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > Proper Configuration for all applications and frameworks

control_title: Verify that the application, configuration, and all dependencies can be re-deployed using automated deployment scripts, built from a documented and tested runbook in a reasonable time, or restored from backups in a timely fashion.
hub_path: Development processes for security > Deploy/build > Ensure repeatability of deployment

control_title: Verify that authorized administrators can verify the integrity of all security-relevant configurations to detect tampering.
hub_name: Set and confirm integrity of security deployment configuration
hub_path: Development processes for security > Deploy/build > Set and confirm integrity of security deployment configuration

control_title: Verify that all components are up to date, preferably using a dependency checker during build or compile time.
hub_name: Update third party components build- or compile time
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency management > Update third party components build- or compile time

control_title: Verify that all unneeded features, documentation, sample applications and configurations are removed.
hub_name: Remove unnecessary elements from external components (e.g. features, documentation, configuration)
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Remove unnecessary elements from external components (e.g. features, documentation, configuration)

control_title: Verify that if application assets, such as JavaScript libraries, CSS or web fonts, are hosted externally on a Content Delivery Network (CDN) or external provider, Subresource Integrity (SRI) is used to validate the integrity of the asset.
hub_name: Enforce integrity check for externally hosted assets (eg SRI)
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency integrity > Enforce integrity check for externally hosted assets (eg SRI)

control_title: Verify that third party components come from pre-defined, trusted and continually maintained repositories.
hub_name: Ensure trusted origin of third party resources
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency integrity > Ensure trusted origin of third party resources

control_title: Verify that a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) is maintained of all third party libraries in use.
hub_name: Maintain/manage inventory of third party components
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency management > Maintain/manage inventory of third party components

control_title: Verify that the attack surface is reduced by sandboxing or encapsulating third party libraries to expose only the required behaviour into the application.
hub_path: Development processes for security > Supply chain management > Dependency integrity > Sandbox third party libraries

control_title: Verify that web or application server and application framework debug modes are disabled in production to eliminate debug features, developer consoles, and unintended security disclosures.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Prevent security disclosure > Disable debug mode in production

control_title: Verify that the HTTP headers or any part of the HTTP response do not expose detailed version information of system components.
hub_name: Do not disclose technical information in HTTP header or response
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Prevent security disclosure > Do not disclose technical information in HTTP header or response

control_title: Verify that every HTTP response contains a Content-Type header. Also specify a safe character set (e.g., UTF-8, ISO-8859-1) if the content types are text/*, /+xml and application/xml. Content must match with the provided Content-Type header.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > HTTP security headers > Set content HTTP response type

control_title: Verify that all API responses contain a Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="api.json" header (or other appropriate filename for the content type).
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > HTTP security headers > Set metadata/content-Disposition for API responses

control_title: Verify that a Content Security Policy (CSP) response header is in place that helps mitigate impact for XSS attacks like HTML, DOM, JSON, and JavaScript injection vulnerabilities.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > HTTP security headers > Configure CSP configuration properly

control_title: Verify that all responses contain a X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > HTTP security headers > Configure X-Content-Type-Options properly

control_title: Verify that a Strict-Transport-Security header is included on all responses and for all subdomains, such as Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=15724800; includeSubdomains.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > HTTP security headers > Configure HSTS configuration properly

control_title: Verify that a suitable Referrer-Policy header is included to avoid exposing sensitive information in the URL through the Referer header to untrusted parties.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > HTTP security headers > Configure Referrer-Policy properly

control_title: Verify that the content of a web application cannot be embedded in a third-party site by default and that embedding of the exact resources is only allowed where necessary by using suitable Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors and X-Frame-Options response headers.
hub_name: Prevent Click jacking through X-Frame-Options or CSP
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Configuration hardening > HTTP security headers > Prevent Click jacking through X-Frame-Options or CSP

control_title: Verify that the application server only accepts the HTTP methods in use by the application/API, including pre-flight OPTIONS, and logs/alerts on any requests that are not valid for the application context.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Validate HTTP request headers > White-list HTTP methods

control_title: Verify that the supplied Origin header is not used for authentication or access control decisions, as the Origin header can easily be changed by an attacker.
hub_name: Avoid using of Origin header for authentication of access control
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Validate HTTP request headers > Avoid using of Origin header for authentication of access control

control_title: Verify that the Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) Access-Control-Allow-Origin header uses a strict allow list of trusted domains and subdomains to match against and does not support the "null" origin.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Validate HTTP request headers > Whitelist CORS resources

control_title: Verify that HTTP headers added by a trusted proxy or SSO devices, such as a bearer token, are authenticated by the application.
hub_name: Authenticate HTTP headers added by a trusted proxy or SSO device
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Validate HTTP request headers > Authenticate HTTP headers added by a trusted proxy or SSO device

control_title: Verify that user set passwords are at least 12 characters in length (after multiple spaces are combined).
hub_name: Enforce user passwords are of sufficient minimum length
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Credentials directives > Enforce user passwords are of sufficient minimum length

control_title: Verify that there are no periodic credential rotation or password history requirements.
hub_name: Do not enforce password rotation rules or history requirements
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Credentials directives > Do not enforce password rotation rules or history requirements

control_title: Verify that "paste" functionality, browser password helpers, and external password managers are permitted.
hub_name: Allow password helpers, including paste functionality
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Login functionality > Allow password helpers, including paste functionality

control_title: Verify that the user can choose to either temporarily view the entire masked password, or temporarily view the last typed character of the password on platforms that do not have this as built-in functionality.
hub_name: Provide options to view entire password or last typed character
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Login functionality > Provide options to view entire password or last typed character

control_title: Verify that passwords of at least 64 characters are permitted, and that passwords of more than 128 characters are denied.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Credentials directives > Allow long passwords

control_title: Verify that password truncation is not performed. However, consecutive multiple spaces may be replaced by a single space.
hub_name: Avoid password truncation, with exception of consecutive spaces
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Credentials directives > Avoid password truncation, with exception of consecutive spaces

control_title: Verify that any printable Unicode character, including language neutral characters such as spaces and Emojis are permitted in passwords.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Credentials directives > Allow unicode in passwords

control_title: Verify users can change their password.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure user management > Offer password changing functionality

control_title: Verify that password change functionality requires the user's current and new password.
hub_name: Change password with presence of old and new password
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure user management > Change password with presence of old and new password

control_title: Verify that passwords submitted during account registration, login, and password change are checked against a set of breached passwords either locally (such as the top 1,000 or 10,000 most common passwords which match the system's password policy) or using an external API. If using an API a zero knowledge proof or other mechanism should be used to ensure that the plain text password is not sent or used in verifying the breach status of the password. If the password is breached, the application must require the user to set a new non-breached password. ([C6](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))
hub_name: Validate new passwords are not in commonly breached passwords list
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Credentials directives > Validate new passwords are not in commonly breached passwords list

control_title: Verify that a password strength meter is provided to help users set a stronger password.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Credentials directives > Provide a password strength meter

control_title: Verify that there are no password composition rules limiting the type of characters permitted. There should be no requirement for upper or lower case or numbers or special characters.
hub_name: Do not limit character types for password composition
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Credentials directives > Do not limit character types for password composition

control_title: Verify that intra-service secrets do not rely on unchanging credentials such as passwords, API keys or shared accounts with privileged access.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure user management > Do not use static secrets

control_title: Verify that if passwords are required for service authentication, the service account used is not a default credential. (e.g. root/root or admin/admin are default in some services during installation).
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure user management > Disallow default credentials

control_title: Verify that passwords are stored with sufficient protection to prevent offline recovery attacks, including local system access.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Store credentials securely

control_title: Verify passwords, integrations with databases and third-party systems, seeds and internal secrets, and API keys are managed securely and not included in the source code or stored within source code repositories. Such storage SHOULD resist offline attacks. The use of a secure software key store (L1), hardware TPM, or an HSM (L3) is recommended for password storage.
hub_name: Do not store secrets in the code
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Do not store secrets in the code

control_title: Verify that anti-automation controls are effective at mitigating breached credential testing, brute force, and account lockout attacks. Such controls include blocking the most common breached passwords, soft lockouts, rate limiting, CAPTCHA, ever increasing delays between attempts, IP address restrictions, or risk-based restrictions such as location, first login on a device, recent attempts to unlock the account, or similar. Verify that no more than 100 failed attempts per hour is possible on a single account.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Restrict excessive authentication

control_title: Verify that the use of weak authenticators (such as SMS and email) is limited to secondary verification and transaction approval and not as a replacement for more secure authentication methods. Verify that stronger methods are offered before weak methods, users are aware of the risks, or that proper measures are in place to limit the risks of account compromise.
hub_name: Prioritize strong multi-factor authenticators  (e.g. NOT SMS/mail) for critical access
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Prioritize strong multi-factor authenticators  (e.g. NOT SMS/mail) for critical access

control_title: Verify that secure notifications are sent to users after updates to authentication details, such as credential resets, email or address changes, logging in from unknown or risky locations. The use of push notifications - rather than SMS or email - is preferred, but in the absence of push notifications, SMS or email is acceptable as long as no sensitive information is disclosed in the notification.
hub_name: Notify users about anomalies in their usage patterns
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure user management > Notify users about anomalies in their usage patterns

control_title: Verify impersonation resistance against phishing, such as the use of multi-factor authentication, cryptographic devices with intent (such as connected keys with a push to authenticate), or at higher AAL levels, client-side certificates.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Resist stolen credentials

control_title: Verify that where a Credential Service Provider (CSP) and the application verifying authentication are separated, mutually authenticated TLS is in place between the two endpoints.
hub_name: Mutually authenticate application and credential service provider
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Mutually authenticate application and credential service provider

control_title: Verify replay resistance through the mandated use of One-time Passwords (OTP) devices, cryptographic authenticators, or lookup codes.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Mandate using multi factor authentication

control_title: Verify intent to authenticate by requiring the entry of an OTP token or user-initiated action such as a button press on a FIDO hardware key.
hub_name: Authenticate by OTP token entry or user-initiated action on multi factor device
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Authenticate by OTP token entry or user-initiated action on multi factor device

control_title: Verify system generated initial passwords or activation codes SHOULD be securely randomly generated, SHOULD be at least 6 characters long, and MAY contain letters and numbers, and expire after a short period of time. These initial secrets must not be permitted to become the long term password.
hub_name: Generate initial passwords with sufficient secure random, short expiration time and do not allow to reuse the initial password.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Generate initial passwords with sufficient secure random, short expiration time and do not allow to reuse the initial password.

control_title: Verify that enrollment and use of user-provided authentication devices are supported, such as a U2F or FIDO tokens.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Support subscriber-provided authentication devices

control_title: Verify that renewal instructions are sent with sufficient time to renew time bound authenticators.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure user management > Inform users for authentication renewal

control_title: Verify that passwords are stored in a form that is resistant to offline attacks. Passwords SHALL be salted and hashed using an approved one-way key derivation or password hashing function. Key derivation and password hashing functions take a password, a salt, and a cost factor as inputs when generating a password hash.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Store passwords salted and hashed

control_title: Verify that the salt is at least 32 bits in length and be chosen arbitrarily to minimize salt value collisions among stored hashes. For each credential, a unique salt value and the resulting hash SHALL be stored.
hub_name: Use unique random salt with sufficient entropy for each credential
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Use unique random salt with sufficient entropy for each credential

control_title: Verify that if PBKDF2 is used, the iteration count SHOULD be as large as verification server performance will allow, typically at least 100,000 iterations.
hub_name: Set the highest feasible iteration count for PBKDF2
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Set the highest feasible iteration count for PBKDF2

control_title: Verify that if bcrypt is used, the work factor SHOULD be as large as verification server performance will allow, with a minimum of 10.
hub_name: Set the highest feasible work factor for bcrypt
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Set the highest feasible work factor for bcrypt

control_title: Verify that an additional iteration of a key derivation function is performed, using a salt value that is secret and known only to the verifier. Generate the salt value using an approved random bit generator [SP 800-90Ar1] and provide at least the minimum security strength specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A. The secret salt value SHALL be stored separately from the hashed passwords (e.g., in a specialized device like a hardware security module).
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Use separately stored secret salt (pepper)

control_title: Verify that a system generated initial activation or recovery secret is not sent in clear text to the user.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Credential recovery > Send authentication secrets encrypted

control_title: Verify password hints or knowledge-based authentication (so-called "secret questions") are not present.
hub_name: Do not use password hints or secret questions
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Credential recovery > Do not use password hints or secret questions

control_title: Verify password credential recovery does not reveal the current password in any way.
hub_name: Do not reveal the current password during password recovery
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Credential recovery > Do not reveal the current password during password recovery

control_title: Verify shared or default accounts are not present (e.g. "root", "admin", or "sa").
hub_path: Operating processes for security > Access control processes > Disallow shared high privileged accounts

control_title: Verify that if an authentication factor is changed or replaced, that the user is notified of this event.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure user management > Notify user about credential change

control_title: Verify forgotten password, and other recovery paths use a secure recovery mechanism, such as time-based OTP (TOTP) or other soft token, mobile push, or another offline recovery mechanism.
hub_name: Use secure recovery mechanisms for forgotten passwords
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Credential recovery > Use secure recovery mechanisms for forgotten passwords

control_title: Verify that if OTP or multi-factor authentication factors are lost, that evidence of identity proofing is performed at the same level as during enrollment.
hub_name: Require proof of identity of the same level as during enrollment when recovering OTP or MFA
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Credential recovery > Require proof of identity of the same level as during enrollment when recovering OTP or MFA

control_title: Verify that lookup secrets can be used only once.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Use lookup secrets only once

control_title: Verify that lookup secrets have sufficient randomness (112 bits of entropy), or if less than 112 bits of entropy, salted with a unique and random 32-bit salt and hashed with an approved one-way hash.
hub_name: Generate multi-factor lookup secrets with sufficient entropy
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Generate multi-factor lookup secrets with sufficient entropy

control_title: Verify that lookup secrets are resistant to offline attacks, such as predictable values.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Use unpredictable multi-factor lookup secrets

control_title: Verify that clear text out of band (NIST "restricted") authenticators, such as SMS or PSTN, are not offered by default, and stronger alternatives such as push notifications are offered first.
hub_name: Do not offer weak (clear text) multi-factor authenticators by default
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Do not offer weak (clear text) multi-factor authenticators by default

control_title: Verify that the out of band verifier expires out of band authentication requests, codes, or tokens after 10 minutes.
hub_name: Ensure timely expiration of out of band authentication request, code, or tokens
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Ensure timely expiration of out of band authentication request, code, or tokens

control_title: Verify that the out of band verifier authentication requests, codes, or tokens are only usable once, and only for the original authentication request.
hub_name: Use out of band authentication requests, codes or tokens only once
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Use out of band authentication requests, codes or tokens only once

control_title: Verify that the out of band authenticator and verifier communicates over a secure independent channel.
hub_name: Communicate out of band multi factor authentication requests, codes or tokens independently and securely
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Communicate out of band multi factor authentication requests, codes or tokens independently and securely

control_title: Verify that the out of band verifier retains only a hashed version of the authentication code.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Only store hashed authentication codes

control_title: Verify that the initial authentication code is generated by a secure random number generator, containing at least 20 bits of entropy (typically a six digital random number is sufficient).
hub_name: Use secure random to generate initial authentication codes
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Use secure random to generate initial authentication codes

control_title: Verify that time-based OTPs have a defined lifetime before expiring.
hub_name: Defined lifetime of time-based one-time password
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Defined lifetime of time-based one-time password

control_title: Verify that symmetric keys used to verify submitted OTPs are highly protected, such as by using a hardware security module or secure operating system based key storage.
hub_name: Use security module to store one-time password verification keys
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Use security module to store one-time password verification keys

control_title: Verify that approved cryptographic algorithms are used in the generation, seeding, and verification of OTPs.
hub_name: Use approved cryptographic algorithms in generation, seeding and verification of OTPs
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Use approved cryptographic algorithms in generation, seeding and verification of OTPs

control_title: Verify that time-based OTP can be used only once within the validity period.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Use time-based OTP only once

control_title: Verify that if a time-based multi-factor OTP token is re-used during the validity period, it is logged and rejected with secure notifications being sent to the holder of the device.
hub_name: Log and reject re-use of valid time-based OTP tokens and notify device holder.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Log and reject re-use of valid time-based OTP tokens and notify device holder.

control_title: Verify physical single-factor OTP generator can be revoked in case of theft or other loss. Ensure that revocation is immediately effective across logged in sessions, regardless of location.
hub_name: Ensure that physical single factor OTP generator can be revoked fully immediately when lost
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Ensure that physical single factor OTP generator can be revoked fully immediately when lost

control_title: Verify that biometric authenticators are limited to use only as secondary factors in conjunction with either something you have and something you know.
hub_name: Biometric authenticators only as secondary factors
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > MFA/OTP > Biometric authenticators only as secondary factors

control_title: Verify that cryptographic keys used in verification are stored securely and protected against disclosure, such as using a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) or Hardware Security Module (HSM), or an OS service that can use this secure storage.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Secure data storage > Secret storage > Store cryptographic keys securely

control_title: Verify that the challenge nonce is at least 64 bits in length, and statistically unique or unique over the lifetime of the cryptographic device.
hub_name: Use a unique challenge nonce of sufficient size
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Challenge nonce cryptography > Use a unique challenge nonce of sufficient size

control_title: Verify that approved cryptographic algorithms are used in the generation, seeding, and verification.
hub_name: Use approved cryptographic algorithms for generation, seeding and verification
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Authentication > Authentication mechanism > Challenge nonce cryptography > Use approved cryptographic algorithms for generation, seeding and verification

control_title: Verify the application never reveals session tokens in URL parameters.
hub_name: Do not expose session token in URL
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Do not expose session token in URL

control_title: Verify the application generates a new session token on user authentication.
hub_name: Generate a new session token after authentication
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Session token generation > Generate a new session token after authentication

control_title: Verify that session tokens possess at least 64 bits of entropy.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Session token generation > Enforce high entropy session tokens

control_title: Verify the application only stores session tokens in the browser using secure methods such as appropriately secured cookies (see section 3.4) or HTML 5 session storage.
hub_name: When storing session tokens in browser, use secure methods only
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Session token generation > When storing session tokens in browser, use secure methods only

control_title: Verify that session tokens are generated using approved cryptographic algorithms.
hub_name: Ensure secure algorithms for generating session tokens
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Session token generation > Ensure secure algorithms for generating session tokens

control_title: Verify that logout and expiration invalidate the session token, such that the back button or a downstream relying party does not resume an authenticated session, including across relying parties.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Minimize session life > Terminate session after logout

control_title: If authenticators permit users to remain logged in, verify that re-authentication occurs periodically both when actively used or after an idle period.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Minimize session life > Ensure session timeout (soft/hard)

control_title: Verify that the application gives the option to terminate all other active sessions after a successful password change (including change via password reset/recovery), and that this is effective across the application, federated login (if present), and any relying parties.
hub_name: Terminate all sessions when password is changed
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Minimize session life > Terminate all sessions when password is changed

control_title: Verify that users are able to view and (having re-entered login credentials) log out of any or all currently active sessions and devices.
hub_name: Enable option to log out from all active session
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Minimize session life > Enable option to log out from all active session

control_title: Verify that cookie-based session tokens have the 'Secure' attribute set.
hub_name: Set "secure" attribute for cookie-based session tokens
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Cookie-config > Set "secure" attribute for cookie-based session tokens

control_title: Verify that cookie-based session tokens have the 'HttpOnly' attribute set.
hub_name: Set httponly attribute for cookie-based session tokens
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Cookie-config > Set httponly attribute for cookie-based session tokens

control_title: Verify that cookie-based session tokens utilize the 'SameSite' attribute to limit exposure to cross-site request forgery attacks.
hub_name: Set "samesite" attribute for cookie-based session tokens
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Cookie-config > Set "samesite" attribute for cookie-based session tokens

control_title: Verify that cookie-based session tokens use the "__Host-" prefix so cookies are only sent to the host that initially set the cookie.
hub_name: Set '_Host'  prefix for cookie-based session tokens
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Cookie-config > Set '_Host'  prefix for cookie-based session tokens

control_title: Verify that if the application is published under a domain name with other applications that set or use session cookies that might disclose the session cookies, set the path attribute in cookie-based session tokens using the most precise path possible.
hub_name: Set path attribute in cookie-based session tokens as precise as possible
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Cookie-config > Set path attribute in cookie-based session tokens as precise as possible

control_title: Verify the application allows users to revoke OAuth tokens that form trust relationships with linked applications.
hub_name: Allow user revocation of Oauth tokens
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Re-authentication from federation or assertion > Allow user revocation of Oauth tokens

control_title: Verify the application uses session tokens rather than static API secrets and keys, except with legacy implementations.
hub_name: Use ephemeral secrets rather than static secrets
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Session integrity > Use ephemeral secrets rather than static secrets

control_title: Verify that stateless session tokens use digital signatures, encryption, and other countermeasures to protect against tampering, enveloping, replay, null cipher, and key substitution attacks.
hub_name: When using stateless tokens, ensure cryptographically secure characteristics
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Session integrity > When using stateless tokens, ensure cryptographically secure characteristics

control_title: Verify that Relying Parties (RPs) specify the maximum authentication time to Credential Service Providers (CSPs) and that CSPs re-authenticate the user if they haven't used a session within that period.
hub_name: Enforce authentication timeout when dealing with an authentication third party (CSP)
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Re-authentication from federation or assertion > Enforce authentication timeout when dealing with an authentication third party (CSP)

control_title: Verify that Credential Service Providers (CSPs) inform Relying Parties (RPs) of the last authentication event, to allow RPs to determine if they need to re-authenticate the user.
hub_name: When using an authentication third party (CSP), relay last authentication event to other parties in the chain
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Re-authentication from federation or assertion > When using an authentication third party (CSP), relay last authentication event to other parties in the chain

control_title: Verify the application ensures a full, valid login session or requires re-authentication or secondary verification before allowing any sensitive transactions or account modifications.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Session management > Re-authenticate before sensitive transactions

control_title: Verify that the application enforces access control rules on a trusted service layer, especially if client-side access control is present and could be bypassed.
hub_name: Enforce access control on trusted service layer
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Enforce access control on trusted service layer

control_title: Verify that all user and data attributes and policy information used by access controls cannot be manipulated by end users unless specifically authorized.
hub_name: Limit modification of access controls to specifically authorized actors/users
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Minimize permissions > Limit modification of access controls to specifically authorized actors/users

control_title: Verify that the principle of least privilege exists - users should only be able to access functions, data files, URLs, controllers, services, and other resources, for which they possess specific authorization. This implies protection against spoofing and elevation of privilege. ([C7](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Minimize permissions > Enforce least privilege

control_title: Verify that access controls fail securely including when an exception occurs.
hub_name: Ensure that secure fail-safe is in place for access control
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Ensure that secure fail-safe is in place for access control

control_title: Verify that sensitive data and APIs are protected against Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) attacks targeting creation, reading, updating and deletion of records, such as creating or updating someone else's record, viewing everyone's records, or deleting all records.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Protect API against unauthorized access/modification (IDOR)

control_title: Verify that the application or framework enforces a strong anti-CSRF mechanism to protect authenticated functionality, and effective anti-automation or anti-CSRF protects unauthenticated functionality.
hub_name: Use CSRF protection against authenticated functionality, add anti-automation controls for unauthenticated functionality
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Use CSRF protection against authenticated functionality, add anti-automation controls for unauthenticated functionality

control_title: Verify administrative interfaces use appropriate multi-factor authentication to prevent unauthorized use.
hub_name: Use multifactor authentication on administrative interfaces
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Use multifactor authentication on administrative interfaces

control_title: Verify that directory browsing is disabled unless deliberately desired. Additionally, applications should not allow discovery or disclosure of file or directory metadata, such as Thumbs.db, .DS_Store, .git or .svn folders.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Protect against directory browsing/discovery attacks

control_title: Verify the application has additional authorization (such as step up or adaptive authentication) for lower value systems, and / or segregation of duties for high value applications to enforce anti-fraud controls as per the risk of application and past fraud.
hub_name: Enforce additional authorization and segregation of duties
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Technical application access control > Strong authorization checking > Enforce additional authorization and segregation of duties

control_title: Verify that the application has defenses against HTTP parameter pollution attacks, particularly if the application framework makes no distinction about the source of request parameters (GET, POST, cookies, headers, or environment variables).
hub_name: Validatie/enforce HTTP inputs (against HTTP parameter pollution attacks)
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Input validation > Validatie/enforce HTTP inputs (against HTTP parameter pollution attacks)

control_title: Verify that frameworks protect against mass parameter assignment attacks, or that the application has countermeasures to protect against unsafe parameter assignment, such as marking fields private or similar.
hub_name: Protect against mass parameter assignment attack
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Input validation > Protect against mass parameter assignment attack

control_title: Verify that all input (HTML form fields, REST requests, URL parameters, HTTP headers, cookies, batch files, RSS feeds, etc) is validated using positive validation (allow lists).
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Input validation > Whitelist all external (HTTP) input

control_title: Verify that structured data is strongly typed and validated against a defined schema including allowed characters, length and pattern (e.g. credit card numbers, e-mail addresses, telephone numbers, or validating that two related fields are reasonable, such as checking that suburb and zip/postcode match).
hub_name: Enforce schema on type/contents of structured data
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Input validation > Enforce schema on type/contents of structured data

control_title: Verify that URL redirects and forwards only allow destinations which appear on an allow list, or show a warning when redirecting to potentially untrusted content.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Input validation > Whitelist redirected/forwarded URLs

control_title: Verify that all untrusted HTML input from WYSIWYG editors or similar is properly sanitized with an HTML sanitizer library or framework feature. ([C5](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Sanitization and sandboxing > Sanitize untrusted HTML input

control_title: Verify that unstructured data is sanitized to enforce safety measures such as allowed characters and length.
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Sanitization and sandboxing > Sanitize unstructured data

control_title: Verify that the application sanitizes user input before passing to mail systems to protect against SMTP or IMAP injection.
hub_name: Sanitize user input before passing content to mail systems (SMTP/IMAP injection)
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Sanitization and sandboxing > Sanitize user input before passing content to mail systems (SMTP/IMAP injection)

control_title: Verify that the application avoids the use of eval() or other dynamic code execution features. Where there is no alternative, any user input being included must be sanitized or sandboxed before being executed.
hub_name: Do not use eval or dynamic code execution functions
hub_path: Technical application security controls > Input and output protection > Sanitization and sandboxing > Do not use eval or dynamic code execution functions

control_title: Verify that the application protects against template injection attacks by ensuring that any user input being included is sanitized or sandboxed.
hub_name: Sanitize/sandbox user input where template-injection is a threat
